name: create_contact
description: Creates a new contact record in the CRM system with personal information, company association, and optional metadata tags. Returns the newly created contact ID on success.

first_name.$comment: Stored separately from last_name to support flexible name formatting across locales.

last_name.$comment: Separate field from first_name enables proper alphabetical sorting by surname in contact lists.

email.description: The contact's primary email address. Must be a valid email format.
email.$comment: Used as the primary deduplication key; creating a contact with an existing email may trigger a merge or conflict error.

phone.description: The contact's phone number in E.164 international format (e.g., +12125551234).
phone.$comment: E.164 format enforced to ensure consistent parsing and international compatibility for SMS and call tools.

company.description: Name of the company or organization the contact is associated with.
company.$comment: Free-text company name; for linking to a CRM company entity, use a separate associate_company tool.

job_title.description: The contact's job title or role within their organization.
job_title.$comment: Used for segmentation and personalization in sales workflows.

tags.description: List of string labels to categorize and segment this contact for filtering and campaigns.
tags.$comment: Tags are user-defined and case-insensitive. Normalized to lowercase on storage.

custom_fields.description: Optional key-value map of custom CRM fields specific to the organization's contact schema.
custom_fields.$comment: Keys must match field names registered in the CRM schema. Unknown keys may be silently ignored or raise a validation error.
